==Biography==
Shan "'''Chaox'''" Huang was originally born in Chengdu, China and lived in Nanjing, China until the age of seven, when his family moved to Montreal, Canada. At the age of 13, his family moved again to Long Island, New York. While playing for TSM, Huang lived in the TSM gaming house, first in New York and later in California. After his departure from Team SoloMid, Huang moved back to his home in New York.

==League of Legends Career==
===TSM Era and Departure===
Chaox first started gaming at the age of 3 on the SNES, and later on became a semi-competitive player in Dota, World of Warcraft, and Heroes of Newerth before becoming a professional League of Legends player. He first played League of Legends during the beta, but disliked it at first, feeling it wasn't up to par with DotA. After the announcement of the Season 1 prizepool, he stuck with the game.  He was recruited to Team SoloMid in January 2011 after playing with and against [[Reginald]] in solo queue. At first, he was [[Team SoloMid|Team SoloMid's]] support player, but then after [[Locodoco]]'s departure from SoloMid, Chaox became the starting AD carry, a position he would hold for over two years. With TSM, Chaox placed first in several online and offline events and became one of the most well-known AD Carry players in the world.

On March 25, 2013, Chaox was released from [[Team SoloMid]] and replaced by [[WildTurtle]]. Support player [[Xpecial]] claimed the release was due to differences between Chaox and TSM's midlaner [[Reginald]]; however after Wildturtle's impressive performance with TSM, every member of the team agreed they would rather have WildTurtle play instead of Chaox.<ref>[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DE1ROcXr_NQ Xpecial - My Side] ''youtube.com''</ref><ref>[http://www.newsoflegends.com/index.php/tsmwildturtle-6153/ WildTurtle Replacing Chaox as Starter on TSM] ''newsoflegends.com''</ref>

===Trip to Asia and Retirement===
After leaving Team SoloMid, Chaox visited China to learn more about e-Sports. He planned to leave on April 14, 2013 and have the trip to last for two months: one month in China and another month in Hong Kong, Taiwan, Singapore and Korea.<ref>[http://www.twitch.tv/chaoxlol E-sports trip to Asia information below the stream screen] ''twitch.tv''</ref> Unfortunately his trip was delayed because his mother burned his visa,<ref>[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kNSsYM5H3CU REASON WHY TRIP IS DELAYED] ''youtube.com''</ref> but on April 23, Chaox finally arrived Shanghai, China.<ref>[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4qY-cY3gu20 Arrived in Shanghai, China] ''youtube.com''</ref>

While in Asia, Chaox spent time with multiple organizations, including [[Vici Gaming]], [[Invictus Gaming]], and Garena. He also appeared on [[2013 LPL Spring/Regular Season|Tencent LoL Pro League 2013]] as a guest.<ref>[http://v.qq.com/page/f/1/v/f011304zj1v.html?start=103 LOL职业联赛春季赛 互动 ChaoX来到现场] ''v.qq.com''</ref> On June 20, 2013, Chaox arrived back in North America.<ref>[http://www.facebook.com/Chaoxlol/posts/541133405934288 Chaox's Facebook post] ''facebook.com''</ref>

On June 21, 2013, Chaox announced his retirement from competitive League of Legends in a vlog on his YouTube channel, <ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rfkKACUTdXU Announcement Vlogu] youtube.com</ref>. On August 20 and 21st, Chaox represented UNICEF in the eBay Charity Invitational 1v1 tournament<ref>[http://www.ebaycharity.gg/#players Player Profiles]''ebaycharity.gg''</ref>. He defeated [[OneBadBrad]] but lost to [[fabbbyyy]] in the semifinals on the next day.

===Return to competitive League of Legends===
On October 6, 2013, Chaox announced that he is officially looking for a competitive team. <ref>[https://twitter.com/ChaoxLoL/status/387069924835078144 Chaox's Twitter post] ''twitter.com''</ref> His first match since coming out from retirement was as a temporary substitute as AD for [[Napkins in Disguise]] on [[North American Challenger League]] Week 3 Day 3.<ref>[https://www.facebook.com/Chaoxlol/posts/597154180332210 Chaox's facebook post]''facebook.com''</ref> He then tried out for [[Team Coast]], playing as a temporary sub ADC in Challenger Leagues/tournaments that Coast was competing in at the time, such as the [[North American Challenger League|NACL]]<ref>[https://www.facebook.com/TeamCoastGaming/posts/661674893872482 Team Coast with Chaox tonight on ADC vs. Velocity] ''facebook.com''</ref>, however ultimately the position was given to [[WizFujiiN]].

On June 9, 2014, in announcing [[Team Coast]]'s new roster, it was officially revealed that Chaox would become their full-time coach.<ref>[http://teamcoast.net/team-coast-roster-announcement/ Team Coast Roster Announcement]''teamcoast.net''</ref> Chaox moved into Coast's gaming house in Las Vegas <ref>[http://www.ongamers.com/articles/team-coast-announce-new-lol-roster/1100-1697/ Team Coast announce new LoL roster]''ongamers.com''</ref> On July 22, 2014, Chaox decided to step down as the coach for Coast in order to focus on his own competitive play. He mentioned that while he could see coaching as a future career, he is currently trying to climb the ranked ladder and be on the look out for opportunities to return as a competitive player. <ref>[https://www.facebook.com/TeamCoastGaming/posts/797833083589995 Team Coast announcement]''facebook.com''</ref>

On July 29, 2014, it was announced that Chaox would substitute as [[Counter Logic Gaming]]'s AD carry while the main roster bootcamps in Korea. <ref>[http://clgaming.net/news/603-clg-to-bootcamp-in-korea-with-montecristo]''clgaming.net''</ref> He played in four games with a roster consisting entirely of temporary subs.

On October 10, 2014, it was rumored on [[ROBERTxLEE]]'s stream that Chaox would replace him as the starting AD Carry for [[Complexity]].

December 5, Chaox announces he's no longer a free agent - joining [[Roar_(Team)|Roar]]. With the team, he competed in the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Spring Qualifier|NACS 2015 Spring Qualifier]], but they were knocked out by [[Cloud9 G2A]] in the first round. In February 2015, Roar disbanded. Chaox moved to China when Roar reformed two months later and competes in Chinese competitive league.

== Trivia ==
* Attended SUNY Stony Brook, where he studied computer science.<ref>[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=idNrv5mP3Y4 Chaox's Vlog - Week Long Party] ''youtube.com''</ref> <ref>[http://www.solomid.net/forums/index.php?/topic/18433-chaoxs-ama/ Chaox's AMA] ''solomid.net''</ref>
* Played a lot of {{ci|Pantheon}} early in his career.
* His play style on {{ci|Irelia}} was very famous in Season 1 and achieved a nickname of "Best Irelia NA" by Fans from China in Season 1.
* Was an active contributor to the SoloMid.net guides and considers by fans that he is the enlightenment of AD Carry.<ref>[http://solomid.net/searchresults.php?q=chaox+guide SoloMid.net search - Chaox Guide] ''solomid.net''</ref>
* Considered a genius of theorycrafting by [[Dyrus]].<ref>[http://www.reddit.com/r/leagueoflegends/comments/mojso/a_10_minute_dyrus_ama/c32kb5g?context=3 A 10+ Minute Dyrus AMA] ''reddit.com''</ref> 
* At first, streamers humorously pronounced his Summoner name as "Chow-ex". Professional commentators and the public now use "Kay-ox", the intended pronunciation.
* His favorite color is red.
* His first streaming computer was bought for him by his father after he explained what streaming was.
* Plays the trading card games, Magic: The Gathering and Hearthstone recreationally and with other LoL players. 
* Spent $500 dollars on the Hearthstone beta.
* Simultaneously held both ranks 1 and 2 in Solo Queue during Season 3 (before the League system was implemented) on NA servers with ''Chaox'' and his smurf, ''HOY 2 THE WORLD''.
* Rune pages are named after fruits, such as Cantaloupe and Grapes.
* Prefers to only have 1 mastery page that he can change on the fly.
* Based on Chaox's unparalleled last hit skill on minions, he achieved a nickname of "King of Last Hit" by Fans from China.<ref>[http://pc.07073.com/news/gaming/20236.html ChaoX叹息国内电竞圈混乱 赴台发展]''07073.com''</ref>
* His favorite part about Chinese Team Gaming House was the Good Healthy Food, which was cooked by the nanny everyday. <ref>[https://www.facebook.com/TSMChaox/posts/525014850879477 Chaox's Facebook post]''facebook.com''</ref>
* His favorite team is [[KT Rolster Bullets]].
* His all-time favorite AD Carry is {{ci|Ashe}}
* Now dedicates some stream time to provide a lesson to a random subscriber in his channel.
* Was pentakilled by a UPS deliveryman, along with the other members of TSM. <ref>[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vad2fFm2dDA UPS Guy Pentakills TSM (minus Xpecial)] ''youtube.com''</ref>
* His main account is still banned from the LoL forums because of selling lessons back in Season 1.
* While trying out as ADC for [[Team Coast]], he played only {{ci|Caitlyn}} in the [[North American Challenger League|NACL]].
